content.cn_text: 现在翻到第7页的第四部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to section 4, on page 7.

content.cn_text: 第四部分 您将听到一位大学讲师在谈论
content.en_text: Section 4 You will hear a university lecturer talking

content.cn_text: 关于世界上最古老的机械计算机，它被称为安提基特拉机械。
content.en_text: to students about the world's oldest mechanical computer, which is called the anti-kissera mechanism.

content.cn_text: 首先，您有一些时间查看第7页上的问题31到40。
content.en_text: First you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40, on page 7.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答第7页上的问题31到40。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40, on page 7, on page 7, on page 7, on page 7.

content.cn_text: 今天我将谈论有时被描述为世界上最古老的机械计算机——安提基特拉机械。
content.en_text: Today I'm going to talk about what is sometimes described as the world's oldest mechanical computer, the anti-kissera mechanism.

content.cn_text: 这个装置能够计算太阳、月亮和行星在过去或未来的任何日期在天空中的位置。
content.en_text: This device was able to calculate where the sun, moon and planets would be in the sky, on any date in the past or future.

content.cn_text: 它改变了我们对古希腊世界和科学史的理解。
content.en_text: It has transformed our understanding, both of the world of ancient Greece and of the history of science.

content.cn_text: 该机械于1900年在希腊安提基特拉岛附近的海底被发现，因此得名。
content.en_text: The mechanism was discovered in 1900, at the bottom of the sea, off the Greek island of anti-kissera, hence the name by which it's now known.

content.cn_text: 它是一艘大约2100年前沉没的船只的货物的一部分。
content.en_text: It was in the cargo of a ship that sank about 2,100 years ago.

content.cn_text: 潜水员将各种物品带到水面，并将其送到博物馆进行检查。
content.en_text: Divers brought various items to the surface, and they were taken to a museum for examination.

content.cn_text: 其中一件物品被认为是块岩石。然而，几个月后，这个物体开始裂开，博物馆馆长意识到它实际上是人造的，上面覆盖着贝壳和其它海洋物体。
content.en_text: One item was assumed to be a lump of rock. However, a few months later, the object started splitting, and a museum curator realized that it was actually something man-made, covered with shells and other objects from the sea.

content.cn_text: 进一步调查显示，它实际上是某种机械装置，他们决定将其命名为安提基特拉机械。
content.en_text: Further investigation revealed that it was in fact some sort of mechanism, and they decided to call it the anti-kissera mechanism.

content.cn_text: 尽管它严重受损，但仍以三个主要部分和大约80个小碎片的形式幸存下来。
content.en_text: Although it was badly damaged, it had survived in three main parts, and around 80 smaller fragments.

content.cn_text: 安提基特拉机械自发现以来受到了大量研究，尤其是在近年来，当有高度精密的设备可用于分析它时。
content.en_text: The anti-kissera mechanism has been studied a great deal since its discovery, and particularly in recent years, when highly sophisticated equipment has been available to analyse it.

content.cn_text: 一台名为“穹顶”（dome）的机器可以围绕样本，并从略微不同的角度拍摄照片。
content.en_text: One machine, called a dome, can surround a sample and take photographs with the light coming from slightly different angles.

content.cn_text: 这使得机械上的大部分铭文变得清晰可辨，尽管它们已经褪色和磨损。
content.en_text: This has made far more of the inscriptions on the mechanism legible, even though they're faded and worn.

content.cn_text: 另一台先进机器“银翼杀手”（Blade Runner）已被用于拍摄X射线，显示了机械中否则不可见的部件。
content.en_text: Another state of the art machine, the Blade Runner, has been used to take x-rays which show parts of the mechanism that would otherwise be invisible.

content.cn_text: 尽管它重达近8吨，但它被从制造地运到希腊，因为安提基特拉机械太脆弱，无法从那里移动。
content.en_text: Despite weighing nearly 8 tons, it was transported from its place of manufacture to Greece, as the anti-kissera mechanism is too fragile to be moved from there.

content.cn_text: 最初，“银翼杀手”被设计用于检查发动机的裂缝，它的精度可以达到十分之一毫米以下。
content.en_text: The Blade Runner was originally designed to examine engines for cracks, and it can work at an accuracy of less than a tenth of a millimetre.

content.cn_text: 这个装置最初似乎大约33厘米高，17厘米宽，9厘米厚。
content.en_text: It seems the device was originally about 33 centimetres high, 17 centimetres wide, and 9 centimetres thick.

content.cn_text: 它由至少30个金属齿轮组成，这些齿轮相互啮合。
content.en_text: It consisted of at least 30 metal gear wheels, that is, wheels which interlocked with each other.

content.cn_text: 齿轮表面刻有月份名称、设备各部件操作说明以及其他信息。
content.en_text: The surfaces of the gear wheels were inscribed with the names of the months, explanations of how the different parts of the device were to be operated, and other information.

content.cn_text: 这些齿轮又驱动着太阳、月亮以及古希腊人已知的五颗行星的小型可移动模型。
content.en_text: These gear wheels, in turn, operated small movable models of the sun, moon, and the five planets that were known to the ancient Greeks.

content.cn_text: 该机械被认为安装在一个木制框架中。
content.en_text: The mechanism is thought to have been mounted in a frame for which wood was used.

content.cn_text: 那么安提基特拉机械是用来做什么的呢？它显然具有天文功能。
content.en_text: So what was the anti-kissera mechanism used for? It clearly had an astronomical function.

content.cn_text: 使用该设备的人只需旋转一个手柄即可操作齿轮。
content.en_text: The person using the device simply rotated a handle to operate the gear wheels.

content.cn_text: 这些齿轮相互啮合，使太阳、月亮和行星绕地球移动，以显示它们在操作者感兴趣的任何特定日期（可能在未来数年）会如何出现。
content.en_text: These engaged with each other to move the sun, moon, and planets around the earth to show how they would appear on whatever particular date the operator was interested in, which could be years into the future.

content.cn_text: 考虑到古希腊人认为太阳和行星都围绕地球旋转，它们的运动显示得非常准确。
content.en_text: Bearing in mind that the ancient Greeks believed the sun and planets all revolved around the earth, their movements are shown with remarkable accuracy.

content.cn_text: 也许最令人惊讶的是，该机械还能显示何时会发生日食或月食。
content.en_text: Perhaps most astonishing of all, the mechanism also showed when an eclipse was going to take place.

content.cn_text: 这在古代世界非常重要，能够准确预测这一点将赋予使用者巨大的权力。
content.en_text: This was very important in the ancient world, and being able to predict this accurately would have given the user great power.

content.cn_text: 该机械可能还通过充当一种事件日历，帮助规划农业和宗教节日的时机。
content.en_text: The mechanism probably also helped in planning the timing of festivals, both agricultural and religious, by acting as a kind of calendar of events.

content.cn_text: 令人惊讶的是，据我们所知，至少一千年后，没有设备能与安提基特拉机械的复杂性相媲美。
content.en_text: Surprisingly, to the best of our knowledge, no device equaled the complexity of the anti-kissera mechanism for at least a thousand years afterwards.

content.cn_text: 然而，这些思想似乎以某种形式幸存下来，特别是在东地中海的拜占庭帝国。
content.en_text: The ideas seemed to have survived in some form, however, particularly in the Byzantine Empire of the Eastern Mediterranean.

content.cn_text: 大约在13世纪或14世纪，这项技术的知识似乎传到了西欧，并制造了大量的时钟，它们提供天文信息，并显示一天中的时间。
content.en_text: Around the 13th or 14th century, knowledge of the technology seems to have reached Western Europe, and a large number of clocks were made, providing astronomical information as well as showing the time of day.

content.cn_text: 很难相信在一千年里没有任何东西达到安提基特拉机械的复杂程度，但这很可能是事实，这对于我们理解科学知识如何进步具有重要意义。
content.en_text: It's hard to believe that nothing reached the sophistication of the anti-kissera mechanism for a thousand years, yet that might well be the case, and that has implications for our understanding of how scientific knowledge progresses.

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。您现在有半分钟的时间检查答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of section 4. You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: 感谢观看。
content.en_text: Thanks for watching.

content.cn_text: 感谢观看。
content.en_text: Thanks for watching.

content.cn_text: 感谢观看。
content.en_text: Thanks for watching.

groups.desc: Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31-40.

items.title: The Antikythera mechanism was discovered as part of a ship’s 31. ____________ in the sea near Antikythera, Greece.
Initially, it was mistakenly thought to be a piece of 32. ____________, but it was later found to be a mechanism that had broken into pieces.

For its analysis, various equipment was used: the “Dome” produced photographs that made the 33. ____________clearer, and the “Blade-Runner” produced X-rays, originally used to identify 34. ____________ in engines.

For its analysis, various equipment was used: the “Dome” produced photographs that made the 33. ____________clearer, and the “Blade-Runner” produced X-rays, originally used to identify 34. ____________ in engines.
The mechanism consisted of over 30 gear wheels made of 35. ____________, models of the sun, moon, and planets, and a framework made of 36. ____________.
The mechanism consisted of over 30 gear wheels made of 35. ____________, models of the sun, moon, and planets, and a framework made of 36. ____________.
The operator turned a 37. ____________ to move the gear wheels, allowing the sun, moon, and planets to be positioned correctly for any given date.
Most surprisingly, the mechanism could calculate when an 38. ____________ would occur and may have been used as a 39. ____________ for planning festivals.
Most surprisingly, the mechanism could calculate when an 38. ____________ would occur and may have been used as a 39. ____________ for planning festivals.
Later, similar technology was used in the 13th and 14th centuries for making 40. ____________ in Western Europe.

groups.source_collect:
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
The world’s oldest mechanical computer: the Antikythera mechanism
Discovery:
• The Antikythera mechanism was discovered as part of a ship’s $31$ in the sea near Antikythera, Greece.
• Initially, it was mistakenly thought to be a piece of $32$, but it was later found to be a mechanism that had broken into pieces.
Equipment used for analysis of the mechanism:
• For its analysis, various equipment was used: the “Dome” produced photographs that made the $33$clearer, and the “Blade-Runner” produced X-rays, originally used to identify $34$ in engines.
Description:
• The mechanism consisted of over 30 gear wheels made of $35$, models of the sun, moon, and planets, and a framework made of $36$.
How the mechanism was used:
• The operator turned a $37$ to move the gear wheels, allowing the sun, moon, and planets to be positioned correctly for any given date.
• Most surprisingly, the mechanism could calculate when an $38$ would occur and may have been used as a $39$ for planning festivals.
Later use of similar technology:
• Later, similar technology was used in the 13th and 14th centuries for making $40$ in Western Europe.
